mnemonics: G.O.A.L. - Gradient Optimization Always Lowers cost (by moving opposite to the gradient)
keyPoints: The gradient points in the direction of steepest ascent of the cost function.
To minimize cost, move in the direction opposite to the gradient.
The learning rate controls the step size taken in the descent direction.
commonMistakes: Choosing a learning rate that is too large (causing divergence) or too small (causing slow convergence).
Forgetting to normalize or scale features, which can make the cost function contour highly elliptical and hard to optimize.
